text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Choi
MRN: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Medical Oncology / Interventional Radiology
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: Fresh in formalin
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN ID: SP-[REDACTED]-23
PROCEDURE DATE: [DATE]
REPORT STATUS: FINAL

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY:
The patient is a 58-year-old female (at index diagnosis; age 59 at time of this biopsy) with a known history of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) of the head/uncinate process, originally staged as borderline resectable (cT3 N1 M0, Stage III). She underwent multimodality neoadjuvant treatment including 8 cycles of modified FOLFIRINOX followed by consolidative long-course chemoradiation (50.4 Gy in 28 fractions with concurrent cape), followed by pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure). Surgical pathology from Whipple demonstrated ypT2 ypN2 M0 disease with R0 resection; 4 of 24 regional lymph nodes were positive. She subsequently initiated adjuvant gem/cape (GemCap, ESPAC-4 regimen). Midpoint restaging CT imaging during adjuvant therapy identified two indeterminate hepatic hypodensities in the right lobe (segments VI and VII). Follow-up MRI abdomen with hepatobiliary protocol (gadoxetate disodium) demonstrated rim-enhancing lesions with arterial enhancement, washout, and hepatobiliary phase hypointensity, morphologically highly suspicious for hepatic metastases. CT-guided core needle biopsy of the segment VI hepatic lesion (9 mm on MRI) was performed to histologically confirm the nature of the lesion and guide further management. CA 19-9 at the time of biopsy: 58 U/mL (rising from 22 U/mL at start of adjuvant therapy). Referring clinical team: Medical Oncology. Clinical question: Rule out metastatic PDAC versus primary hepatic lesion.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
A: Liver, segment VI, CT-guided core needle biopsy

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "liver biopsy segment VI," are two cores of tan-pink soft tissue, each measuring approximately 1.0 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores are soft to firm on palpation. On gross sectioning, one core demonstrates a subtle pale, firm area comprising approximately 40% of its length, distinct from the adjacent tan hepatic parenchyma. No hemorrhage or necrosis is grossly apparent. Entire specimen submitted in one cassette, A1.

Total cores: 2
Total tissue length: approximately 2.0 cm (combined)
Cassettes: 1 (A1 - entirely submitted)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Section A1 shows two cores of hepatic parenchyma. One core demonstrates partial infiltration by a malignant epithelial neoplasm; the second core contains a small focus of the same process at one end and otherwise shows benign hepatic parenchyma.

The neoplasm is composed of irregular, angulated, and focally cribriform malignant glands set within a densely fibrotic, desmoplastic stroma. The glandular structures are lined by columnar to cuboidal epithelial cells with moderately enlarged, pleomorphic nuclei, irregular nuclear contours, and conspicuous nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (approximately 4 per 10 high-power fields). The cytoplasm is eosinophilic and focally vacuolated; intracytoplasmic and intraluminal mucin is present. Perineural invasion is identified in one section. No lymphovascular invasion is identified in the sampled tissue. The surrounding hepatic parenchyma shows mild sinusoidal dilation, mild macrovesicular steatosis, and no significant fibrosis. No portal inflammation or biliary pathology is identified in the non-neoplastic liver. No granulomata, viral inclusions, or dysplastic hepatocytes are seen. No hepatocellular carcinoma morphology is identified (no hepatocytic differentiation, no bile, no Mallory-Denk bodies, no pseudoacinar pattern consistent with HCC). The overall morphology, including desmoplastic stroma, infiltrating tubular glands, nuclear pleomorphism, and intraluminal mucin production, is consistent with metastatic ductal adenocarcinoma, most consistent with pancreatic primary in the appropriate clinical context.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on block A1):
All immunostains were performed with appropriate positive and negative controls.

  CK7:              POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic glands)
  CK20:             NEGATIVE (no staining in neoplastic cells)
  CDX2:             NEGATIVE (no nuclear staining in neoplastic cells)
  CA19-9:           POSITIVE (moderate to strong cytoplasmic and luminal staining)
  MUC1 (EMA):       POSITIVE (apical and cytoplasmic membrane staining)
  MUC2:             NEGATIVE
  MUC5AC:           POSITIVE (focal cytoplasmic staining)
  SMAD4 (DPC4):     NEGATIVE - LOSS OF EXPRESSION (neoplastic glands show complete absence of nuclear SMAD4 immunoreactivity; internal positive control in stromal fibroblasts and entrapped hepatocytes is intact, confirming assay validity)
  PAX8:             NEGATIVE
  TTF-1:            NEGATIVE
  Napsin A:         NEGATIVE
  SATB2:            NEGATIVE
  HepPar-1:         NEGATIVE
  Arginase-1:       NEGATIVE
  GPC3 (Glypican-3):NEGATIVE
  p40:              NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin:    NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A:   NEGATIVE
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):    Approximately 40% of neoplastic nuclei positive (high proliferative index)

NOTE: SMAD4/DPC4 loss of expression by IHC is concordant with the known SMAD4 p.R361H mutation and IHC loss identified in the primary pancreatic tumor at diagnosis and confirmed in the Whipple resection specimen.

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) PROTEINS (performed on block A1):
  MLH1:   RETAINED (nuclear positive)
  MSH2:   RETAINED (nuclear positive)
  MSH6:   RETAINED (nuclear positive)
  PMS2:   RETAINED (nuclear positive)
  Interpretation: No loss of MMR protein expression detected; findings consistent with proficient mismatch repair (pMMR) / microsatellite stable (MSS) status. This is concordant with the prior NGS result (MSS, TMB 4 mutations/Mb) from the primary tumor.

ADDITIONAL STUDIES PENDING AT TIME OF FINAL SIGN-OUT:
  - FISH for ERBB2 (HER2) amplification: PENDING [results to be issued as addendum]
  - KRAS mutation analysis by single-gene PCR (confirmatory): PENDING [results to be issued as addendum]
  NOTE: Comprehensive molecular profiling (NGS) of this metastatic specimen may be considered at the discretion of the treating oncologist to assess for clonal evolution or emergence of new targetable alterations.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

A. LIVER, SEGMENT VI, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   - METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA
   - Immunohistochemical profile (CK7+, CK20-, CDX2-, CA19-9+, MUC1+, SMAD4/DPC4 loss of expression) is consistent with metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
   - See comment

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The histomorphologic and immunohistochemical findings in this core needle biopsy of a right hepatic lobe mass are diagnostic of metastatic adenocarcinoma. The immunophenotype is characteristic of a pancreatobiliary primary: CK7 positive, CK20 negative, CDX2 negative, with MUC1 and CA19-9 positivity. Most notably, complete loss of SMAD4 (DPC4) nuclear expression is identified in the neoplastic cells, with preserved staining in surrounding stromal and hepatic cells, confirming this as a true loss rather than a technical artifact. This pattern of SMAD4 loss is highly characteristic of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma and is concordant with the SMAD4 p.R361H somatic mutation detected by comprehensive NGS (FoundationOne CDx) in the primary tumor at initial diagnosis, as well as the DPC4 loss confirmed by IHC in the index EUS-FNB specimen and subsequent Whipple resection specimen. A colorectal primary is effectively excluded by the absence of CK20, CDX2, and SATB2 expression. A cholangiocarcinoma primary is possible on IHC grounds alone, but is considered unlikely given the known pancreatic primary, the absence of biliary lesions on serial imaging, and the concordant SMAD4 loss. Hepatocellular carcinoma is excluded by negative HepPar-1, Arginase-1, and GPC3 with intact non-neoplastic hepatocyte staining. A neuroendocrine neoplasm is excluded by negative synaptophysin and chromogranin. The high Ki-67 labeling index (approximately 40%) in the metastatic deposit is somewhat elevated compared to the Ki-67 of approximately 20% noted in residual viable tumor at the time of Whipple resection, suggesting possible clonal selection of more proliferative disease in the metastatic setting, though Ki-67 assessment on small biopsy specimens has inherent sampling variability.

In the appropriate clinical context of a patient with known SMAD4-deficient pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma who developed rising CA 19-9 and new hepatic lesions during adjuvant chemotherapy, the findings confirm hepatic metastasis from pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (M1 disease). This represents progression to stage IV disease occurring during adjuvant GemCap therapy.

Correlation with clinical and radiologic findings is confirmed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST SIGNATURE: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Final report electronically signed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CASE CC: Medical Oncology, Interventional Radiology
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
